root_text.an3.34:1.3: Lobho nidānaṁ kammānaṁ samudayāya, doso nidānaṁ kammānaṁ samudayāya, moho nidānaṁ kammānaṁ samudayāya.
root_text.an3.34:2.1: Yaṁ, bhikkhave, lobhapakataṁ kammaṁ lobhajaṁ lobhanidānaṁ lobhasamudayaṁ, yatthassa attabhāvo nibbattati tattha taṁ kammaṁ vipaccati.
root_text.an3.34:2.2: Yattha taṁ kammaṁ vipaccati tattha tassa kammassa vipākaṁ paṭisaṁvedeti, diṭṭhe vā dhamme upapajja vā apare vā pariyāye.
root_text.an3.34:3.1: Yaṁ, bhikkhave, dosapakataṁ kammaṁ dosajaṁ dosanidānaṁ dosasamudayaṁ, yatthassa attabhāvo nibbattati tattha taṁ kammaṁ vipaccati.
root_text.an3.34:3.2: Yattha taṁ kammaṁ vipaccati tattha tassa kammassa vipākaṁ paṭisaṁvedeti, diṭṭhe vā dhamme upapajja vā apare vā pariyāye.
root_text.an3.34:4.1: Yaṁ, bhikkhave, mohapakataṁ kammaṁ mohajaṁ mohanidānaṁ mohasamudayaṁ, yatthassa attabhāvo nibbattati tattha taṁ kammaṁ vipaccati.
root_text.an3.34:4.2: Yattha taṁ kammaṁ vipaccati tattha tassa kammassa vipākaṁ paṭisaṁvedeti, diṭṭhe vā dhamme upapajja vā apare vā pariyāye.
root_text.an3.34:5.1: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, bījāni akhaṇḍāni apūtīni avātātapahatāni sārādāni sukhasayitāni sukhette suparikammakatāya bhūmiyā nikkhittāni.
root_text.an3.34:5.3: Evassu tāni, bhikkhave, bījāni vuddhiṁ viruḷhiṁ vepullaṁ āpajjeyyuṁ.
root_text.an3.34:5.4: Evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, yaṁ lobhapakataṁ kammaṁ lobhajaṁ lobhanidānaṁ lobhasamudayaṁ, yatthassa attabhāvo nibbattati tattha taṁ kammaṁ vipaccati.
root_text.an3.34:5.5: Yattha taṁ kammaṁ vipaccati tattha tassa kammassa vipākaṁ paṭisaṁvedeti, diṭṭhe vā dhamme upapajja vā apare vā pariyāye.
root_text.an3.34:6.2: yaṁ mohapakataṁ kammaṁ mohajaṁ mohanidānaṁ mohasamudayaṁ, yatthassa attabhāvo nibbattati tattha taṁ kammaṁ vipaccati.
root_text.an3.34:6.3: Yattha taṁ kammaṁ vipaccati tattha tassa kammassa vipākaṁ paṭisaṁvedeti, diṭṭhe vā dhamme upapajja vā apare vā pariyāye.
root_text.an3.34:6.4: Imāni kho, bhikkhave, tīṇi nidānāni kammānaṁ samudayāya.
root_text.an3.34:7.3: Alobho nidānaṁ kammānaṁ samudayāya, adoso nidānaṁ kammānaṁ samudayāya, amoho nidānaṁ kammānaṁ samudayāya.
root_text.an3.34:8.1: Yaṁ, bhikkhave, alobhapakataṁ kammaṁ alobhajaṁ alobhanidānaṁ alobhasamudayaṁ, lobhe vigate evaṁ taṁ kammaṁ pahīnaṁ hoti ucchinnamūlaṁ tālāvatthukataṁ anabhāvaṅkataṁ āyatiṁ anuppādadhammaṁ.
root_text.an3.34:9.1: Yaṁ, bhikkhave, adosapakataṁ kammaṁ adosajaṁ adosanidānaṁ adosasamudayaṁ, dose vigate evaṁ taṁ kammaṁ pahīnaṁ hoti ucchinnamūlaṁ tālāvatthukataṁ anabhāvaṅkataṁ āyatiṁ anuppādadhammaṁ.
root_text.an3.34:10.1: Yaṁ, bhikkhave, amohapakataṁ kammaṁ amohajaṁ amohanidānaṁ amohasamudayaṁ, mohe vigate evaṁ taṁ kammaṁ pahīnaṁ hoti ucchinnamūlaṁ tālāvatthukataṁ anabhāvaṅkataṁ āyatiṁ anuppādadhammaṁ.
root_text.an3.34:11.1: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, bījāni akhaṇḍāni apūtīni avātātapahatāni sārādāni sukhasayitāni.
root_text.an3.34:11.4: Masiṁ karitvā mahāvāte vā ophuṇeyya nadiyā vā sīghasotāya pavāheyya.
root_text.an3.34:11.5: Evassu tāni, bhikkhave, bījāni ucchinnamūlāni tālāvatthukatāni anabhāvaṅkatāni āyatiṁ anuppādadhammāni.
root_text.an3.34:11.6: Evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, yaṁ alobhapakataṁ kammaṁ alobhajaṁ alobhanidānaṁ alobhasamudayaṁ, lobhe vigate evaṁ taṁ kammaṁ pahīnaṁ hoti ucchinnamūlaṁ tālāvatthukataṁ anabhāvaṅkataṁ āyatiṁ anuppādadhammaṁ.
root_text.an3.34:12.2: yaṁ amohapakataṁ kammaṁ amohajaṁ amohanidānaṁ amohasamudayaṁ, mohe vigate evaṁ taṁ kammaṁ pahīnaṁ hoti …pe…
root_text.an3.34:12.4: Imāni kho, bhikkhave, tīṇi nidānāni kammānaṁ samudayāyāti.

translation_text.an3.34:1.1: “Mendicants, there are these three sources that give rise to deeds.
translation_text.an3.34:1.3: Greed, hate, and delusion are sources that give rise to deeds.
translation_text.an3.34:2.1: Any deed that emerges from greed—born, sourced, and originated from greed—ripens where that new incarnation is born.
translation_text.an3.34:2.2: And wherever that deed ripens, its result is experienced—either in the present life, or in the next life, or in some subsequent period.
translation_text.an3.34:3.1: Any deed that emerges from hate—born, sourced, and originated from hate—ripens where that new incarnation is born.
translation_text.an3.34:3.2: And wherever that deed ripens, its result is experienced—either in the present life, or in the next life, or in some subsequent period.
translation_text.an3.34:4.1: Any deed that emerges from delusion—born, sourced, and originated from delusion—ripens where that new incarnation is born.
translation_text.an3.34:4.2: And wherever that deed ripens, its result is experienced—either in the present life, or in the next life, or in some subsequent period.
translation_text.an3.34:5.1: Suppose some seeds were intact, unspoiled, not weather-damaged, fertile, and well-kept. They’re sown in a well-prepared, productive field,
translation_text.an3.34:5.2: and the heavens provide plenty of rain.
translation_text.an3.34:5.3: Then those seeds would grow, increase, and mature.
translation_text.an3.34:5.4: In the same way, any deed that emerges from greed—born, sourced, and originated from greed—ripens where that new incarnation is born.
translation_text.an3.34:5.5: And wherever that deed ripens, its result is experienced—either in the present life, or in the next life, or in some subsequent period.
translation_text.an3.34:6.1: Any deed that emerges from hate …
translation_text.an3.34:6.2: Any deed that emerges from delusion—born, sourced, and originated from delusion—ripens where that new incarnation is born.
translation_text.an3.34:6.3: And wherever that deed ripens, its result is experienced—either in the present life, or in the next life, or in some subsequent period.
translation_text.an3.34:6.4: These are three sources that give rise to deeds.
translation_text.an3.34:7.1: Mendicants, there are these three sources that give rise to deeds.
translation_text.an3.34:7.3: Contentment, love, and understanding are sources that give rise to deeds.
translation_text.an3.34:8.1: Any deed that emerges from contentment—born, sourced, and originated from contentment—is given up when greed is done away with. It’s cut off at the root, made like a palm stump, obliterated, and unable to arise in the future.
translation_text.an3.34:9.1: Any deed that emerges from love—born, sourced, and originated from love—is abandoned when hate is done away with. It’s cut off at the root, made like a palm stump, obliterated, and unable to arise in the future.
translation_text.an3.34:10.1: Any deed that emerges from understanding—born, sourced, and originated from understanding—is abandoned when delusion is done away with. It’s cut off at the root, made like a palm stump, obliterated, and unable to arise in the future.
translation_text.an3.34:11.1: Suppose some seeds were intact, unspoiled, not damaged by wind and sun, fertile, and well-kept.
translation_text.an3.34:11.2: But someone would burn them with fire,
translation_text.an3.34:11.4: and whisk away the ashes in a strong wind, or glide them down a swift stream.
translation_text.an3.34:11.5: Then those seeds would be cut off at the root, made like a palm stump, obliterated, and unable to arise in the future.
translation_text.an3.34:11.6: In the same way, any deed that emerges from contentment—born, sourced, and originated from contentment—is abandoned when greed is done away with. It’s cut off at the root, made like a palm stump, obliterated, and unable to arise in the future.
translation_text.an3.34:12.1: Any deed that emerges from love …
translation_text.an3.34:12.2: Any deed that emerges from understanding—born, sourced, and originated from understanding—is abandoned when delusion is done away with.
translation_text.an3.34:12.3: It’s cut off at the root, made like a palm stump, obliterated, and unable to arise in the future.
translation_text.an3.34:12.4: These are three sources that give rise to deeds.
translation_text.an3.34:13.2: out of greed, hate, or delusion,
translation_text.an3.34:13.5: are to be experienced right here,
translation_text.an3.34:14.3: of the outcome of greed, hate, and delusion,

variant_text.an3.34:2.2: upapajja vā → upapajje vā (bj, sya-all, km); uppajje vā (pts1ed) | apare vā → aparāpare vā (mr)
